message:
feat(mvcc): add zstd/brotli transparent compression + ondisk correctness fixes

MVCC layer now supports transparent zstd and brotli compression for
versioned data. New VersionData::Zstd and VersionData::Brotli variants
store compressed payloads, and a CompressionAlgo enum lets callers select
compression at write time. read_visible() now returns Cow<[u8]> instead
of &[u8] to support on-the-fly decompression without lifetime gymnastics.
resolve_data_with() handles the Cow plumbing throughout the persistence
and sharded layers.

Workspace-level Cargo.toml gains zstd = "0.13" and brotli = "6.0"
dependencies; ffs-mvcc/Cargo.toml activates them.

Ondisk correctness fixes:
- btrfs: leaf item offset calculation now uses absolute position within
  the block rather than relative-to-header-end, matching the kernel's
  btrfs_item_nr_offset() semantics
- ext4: str2hashbuf() rewritten to match the Linux kernel's big-endian
  byte-packing (4 bytes → u32 via shifts, pad with zero bytes not 0x80);
  rec_len_from_disk() now handles 0xFFFF and 0xFFFE special values for
  64 KiB blocks per ext4_rec_len_from_disk() in fs/ext4/ext4.h

Core layer adjustments:
- Ext4AllocState is now destructured where needed instead of pattern-
  matched monolithically
- Orphan inode bitmap marking fix
- resolve_extent returns tuple for cleaner call sites

Tests updated across WAL throughput benchmarks, flush-epoch-guard
integration, and MVCC stress suite to accommodate Cow return types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
